prompts: Meiosis consists of two main stages: meiosis I and meiosis II. Meiosis I involves homologous chromosomes lining up and separating, resulting in two haploid cells. Stages include prophase I, metaphase I, anaphase I, and telophase I, with crossing over occurring in prophase I. Meiosis II resembles mitosis, where sister chromatids separate. It includes prophase II, metaphase II, anaphase II, and telophase II, ending with four genetically distinct haploid gametes. This process ensures genetic diversity through recombination and reductional cell division.
